title: Pleione ustulata
taxonomy: Pleione ustulata is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Pleione and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Pleione is a group of terrestrial orchids native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. These plants are known for their beautiful, delicate flowers and are often used in horticulture. The classification of Pleione ustulata is still under research, but it is widely accepted as a distinct species within the Orchidaceae family.

intro: Pleione ustulata, also known as the "orchid bee flower", is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, contributing to its use in traditional medicine for treating various health issues, such as fever and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleione ustulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
